# Spec: generate-sbom.md §Strict mode checks
# "Component with type: library has no supplier -> warn"
#
# EXPECTED GAP: strict.go:118 reads
#     c.Type == "library" && name == "" || email == ""
# Go operator precedence makes this
#     (c.Type == "library" && name == "") || email == ""
# so any component (library OR NOT) missing .supplier.email is flagged, even
# when the supplier has a name but no email.

exec sbomasm generate sbom -c meta.yaml -i .components.json -o out.json

# NON-LIBRARY with supplier name but no email -> should NOT be flagged per spec
# (the check is scoped to type: library). But it IS flagged today (gap):
stderr 'app-comp@1.0.0 has no supplier'

# LIBRARY with supplier name AND email -> should NOT be flagged. Today the bug
# causes it to be flagged *only* if email is missing, not when both are set.
! stderr 'has-both@1.0.0 has no supplier'

-- meta.yaml --
app:
  name: app
  version: 1.0.0
  primary_purpose: application
  supplier:
    name: Acme
  license:
    id: MIT
-- .components.json --
{
  "schema": "interlynk/component-manifest/v1",
  "components": [
    { "name": "app-comp", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "application",
      "license": "MIT",
      "supplier": {"name": "Acme"},
      "external_references":[{"type":"distribution","url":"https://x"}],
      "hashes":[{"algorithm":"SHA-256","value":"1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111"}] },
    { "name": "has-both", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "library",
      "license": "MIT",
      "supplier": {"name": "Acme", "email": "sec@acme.example"},
      "external_references":[{"type":"distribution","url":"https://x"}],
      "hashes":[{"algorithm":"SHA-256","value":"2222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222"}] }
  ]
}
